slides.title: Why Cats Belong in Tech
slides.text: After extensive research (watching YouTube for 8 hours), we've identified key traits that make cats ideal software engineers.
bullets: They sit on keyboards with purpose and intent
They've been pushing things off tables (deploying to production) for millennia
They sleep 16 hours a day, which is basically on-call rotation
They ignore you when you talk, just like senior engineers in standup
They demand food at 3 AM, proving they understand cron jobs

slides.title: Performance Review Comparison
slides.text: Far far away, behind the word mountains, far from the countries Vokalia and Consonantia, there live the blind texts. Separated they live in Bookmarksgrove right at the coast of the Semantics, a large language ocean. A small river named Duden flows by their place and supplies it with the necessary regelialia. It is a paradisematic country, in which roasted parts of sentences fly into your mouth. Even the all-powerful Pointing has no control about the blind texts it is an almost unorthographic life One day however a small line of blind text by the name of Lorem Ipsum.

slides.title: The Ideal Workspace

image.alt: Cat in a box with laptop

content.text: Studies show that the optimal developer workspace includes:
bullets: A laptop keyboard to sit on
A sunny spot for post-deploy naps
Treats within paw's reach at all times
